chunks.text: End of Semester Information EECS Instructional Computer Accounts End of Semester Information Contents: These Accounts Expire on the first Monday following the last day of finals /home/tmp directories will be deleted after the last day of finals Labs

will be deleted after the last day of finals Labs Close after the last day of finals ; Server Down Times How do I Verify My Account Status? What happens to the files in an expired account? How Do I Get off the Expired List? How can I check my grades?

t off the Expired List? How can I check my grades? These Accounts Expire on the first Monday following the last day of finals at 9am - Instructional class accounts (such as "ee1-xx", "cs9a-zz", etc) - all Instructional non-CS and non-EECS named acc

- all Instructional non-CS and non-EECS named accounts - Instructional named and "cs199" accounts of CS & EECS majors who have graduated or left the University. 'NAMED' ACCOUNTS THAT WILL EXPIRE 'CLASS' ACCOUNTS THAT WILL NOT EXPIRE For info

RE 'CLASS' ACCOUNTS THAT WILL NOT EXPIRE For info about your own account, login to WebAcct . 'Named' and 'cs199' accounts for ongoing CS and EECS students will remain active without any action by the user. /home/tmp directories will be deleted

y the user. /home/tmp directories will be deleted after the last day of finals All files in the /home/tmp directory will be removed. Please read /share/b/pub/removable-media.help for information about backing up your files. Instructional Labs Clo

bout backing up your files. Instructional Labs Close after the last day of finals Server Down Times - Cardkey access to the Instructional labs will terminate at 11:59pm on the last day of finals . - Instructional servers may be down at times for ma

Instructional servers may be down at times for maintenance between the last day of finals, and the first day of the next semester servers: status between semesters: downtime affects these services: cory.eecs, ashby.cs on-line ssh logins; Ubuntu Linu

ory.eecs, ashby.cs on-line ssh logins; Ubuntu Linux eda-1.eecs, eda-4.eecs on-line ssh LINUX logins; Cadence, Synopsys wserver{1-4}.eecs on-line Windows terminal server logins How do I Verify My Account Status? To verify your account status, login to

nt Status? To verify your account status, login to WebAcct . How do I Get off the Expired List? If our records are in error, please email inst@eecs . Or, you can just wait until the start of the next semester, when we will have newer enrollmen

ext semester, when we will have newer enrollment records, and request your account again . What happens to the files in an expired account? For class accounts ("cs61a-aa", "ee122-aa", etc), the files will be saved for 1-2 weeks, archived to tap

es will be saved for 1-2 weeks, archived to tape and deleted. Class account logins are reassigned to new students next semester. For named accounts ("jdoe", 'cs199-aa', etc), the files will be saved for about a month after the start of t

be saved for about a month after the start of the next semester, and the files will be archived to tape before they are deleted. If the student is eligible again for an account next semester, the account can be renewed . BEFORE your acco

, the account can be renewed . BEFORE your account expires, you can copy your files from your Windows and UNIX home directories by transferring them to a USB memory stick. You could also email them to yourself or use a file transfer program.

them to yourself or use a file transfer program. AFTER your account expires, you will not be able to copy the files. For more information, please see - How Do I Copy Files Between Computers? - http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/cgi-bin/pub.cgi?fi

- http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/cgi-bin/pub.cgi?file=file-transfer.help 'CLASS' ACCOUNTS THAT WILL NOT EXPIRE Instructors must notify inst@eecs about any class accounts that need to be extended after the last day of finals . 'NAMED' ACCOUNTS THAT

er the last day of finals . 'NAMED' ACCOUNTS THAT WILL EXPIRE Users can use WebAcct to check on the status of their Instructional UNIX named accounts: login using your CalNet ID or Instructional UNIX account. inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
